view_observations.front: Front view shows the cabinet blockout proportions, countertop band, plinth, vertical door split, and right-side drawer seam. It is useful for blockout review but lacks hardware, bevels, finished materials, and production joinery detail.
view_observations.rear: Rear view reads as a simple flat cabinet back with countertop overhang and plinth. It confirms rear massing but does not show finished back construction or manufacturable detail.
view_observations.left: Left view shows side massing, countertop overhang, plinth, and the front face projecting forward. It remains plain blockout geometry with no panel construction detail.
view_observations.right: Right view shows side massing plus front drawer/door seam thickness at the leading edge. It helps confirm the blockout layout but still lacks hardware and material fidelity.
view_observations.top: Top view clearly shows the countertop footprint and simple sink marker on the left side. It does not prove real sink cut-out construction, UVs, or countertop material quality.
view_observations.three_quarter: Three-quarter view gives the best overall evidence: front panel seams, countertop separation, plinth, side massing, and sink marker are visible together. It is review-worthy as a blockout and still not production art.

strengths: Blockout now communicates cabinet front layout instead of reading as a plain cuboid.
Countertop distinction, plinth, panel seams, and sink marker are visible in committed evidence.
Collision proxy is hidden from render output while remaining validated in the GLB gates.
Render evidence is SHA-pinned and tied to the current candidate SHA.
weaknesses: Materials are placeholder colors only and do not represent production PBR materials.
UV unwraps, textures, bevels, topology cleanup, and mesh hygiene have not been visually accepted.
Hardware, joinery detail, real sink construction, and manufacturability relevance are not represented.
No human production visual sign-off or manufacturing sign-off is recorded.
required_before_promotion: Joinery and construction detail appropriate to the production claim
next_recommended_action: Improve the candidate blockout toward production visual/manufacturing detail, or begin a separate Fusion proof while keeping this visual candidate blockout-only.
